catalog_description: Two-Blueprint pickup and interaction pattern using a Blueprint Interface. The item implements the interface; the player character handles input and calls Interact. BOTH Blueprints must be modified.
tags: pickup interact interactable equip item collect grab hold weapon pickup loot drop attach detach interface blueprint interface input e key overlap

patterns.name: ArchitectureOverview
patterns.description: Pickup systems require modifications to BOTH the item AND the player character. A Blueprint Interface (BPI_Interactable) decouples them.
patterns.notes: CRITICAL RULES: (1) This is a TWO-BLUEPRINT task — you MUST modify both the item and the player character. (2) Input handling (E key) belongs on the player character, NEVER on the item. (3) The item needs a collision component for overlap detection. (4) Complete one Blueprint fully before moving to the next. (5) If the user asks to 'make X a pickup item', that means BOTH the item BP and the player character need changes.
blueprints_involved: BPI_Interactable (BlueprintInterface) — defines the Interact(InteractingActor:Actor) function signature
Item Blueprint (e.g., BP_Gun) — implements interface, has SphereCollision for overlap, contains Pickup/Drop logic
Player Character Blueprint — handles E-key input, finds nearby interactable actors, calls Interact on them

patterns.name: ItemSetup
patterns.description: The item Blueprint needs: SphereCollision (bGenerateOverlapEvents=true), variables (bIsEquipped:Bool, OwnerRef:Actor), BPI_Interactable interface implementation, and Pickup/Drop functions.
patterns.notes: The Interact function (from the interface) branches on bIsEquipped: if false call Pickup(InteractingActor), if true call Drop(). Pickup attaches to player mesh via K2_AttachToComponent (SnapToTarget), sets bIsEquipped=true, disables collision. Drop detaches via K2_DetachFromActor (KeepWorld), sets bIsEquipped=false, re-enables collision. Keep each function under 10 plan_json steps.

steps.note: Add SphereComponent for interaction detection, then add bIsEquipped and OwnerRef variables.
Implement BPI_Interactable interface, then add Pickup(NewOwner:Actor) and Drop() functions. Implement each function ONE AT A TIME with get_recipe + preview_plan_json + apply_plan_json.

patterns.name: PlayerCharacterSetup
patterns.description: The player character handles E-key input and calls Interact on nearby actors that implement BPI_Interactable.
patterns.notes: Add an EquippedItem:Actor variable. In EventGraph: IA_Interact (Enhanced Input) or InputAction E event → GetOverlappingActors → ForEach loop → DoesImplementInterface check for BPI_Interactable → Branch → call Interact message on the actor, passing self as InteractingActor. The interface message call uses op:call with target:Interact — the resolver handles interface dispatch.

steps.note: Add EquippedItem variable to track held item.
Implement interaction logic in EventGraph with plan_json. Use IA_Interact for the input event (Enhanced Input Action).

patterns.name: ToolCallSequence
patterns.description: Complete step-by-step order. Finish each Blueprint fully before moving to the next.
sequence: 1. Create BPI_Interactable interface (blueprint.create type=BlueprintInterface + add_function Interact)
2. Modify item BP — add SphereComponent, variables, implement interface, add Pickup/Drop functions
3. Implement item functions ONE AT A TIME (get_recipe → preview → apply for each)
5. Read player character BP (to see existing structure)
6. Add EquippedItem variable, then implement E-key interaction logic in EventGraph via plan_json
